# UC-22.20.6 · CMMC CM.L2-3.4.1 — Baseline configuration drift detection on CUI systems

## Description

Detects configuration baseline drift on CUI-classified systems using configuration management tool outputs (Chef InSpec, Puppet, Ansible). CMMC CM.L2-3.4.1 requires establishing and maintaining baseline configurations and inventories of organisational systems throughout the system development life cycle.

## Value

Continuously monitors CUI systems for deviations from approved baseline configurations, providing evidence that baselines are actively enforced and drift is detected and remediated.

## Implementation

(1) Verify data sources: Chef InSpec compliance reports (`chef:compliance`), Puppet reports (`puppet:report`), Ansible results (`ansible:result`), `cui_hosts.csv` lookup; (2) Maintain CUI-scoped lookups; (3) Schedule search at compliance-aligned interval; (4) Route findings to ticketing queue; (5) Retain exports per records management policy.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Chef InSpec compliance reports (`chef:compliance`), Puppet reports (`puppet:report`), Ansible results (`ansible:result`), `cui_hosts.csv` lookup.
• Install and configure: Splunk Add-on for Chef (if available), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify that all required data sources are ingesting into Splunk. Maintain the CUI-scoped lookups (cui_hosts.csv, cui_network_segments.csv, etc.) with current asset inventory.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL (save as report or alert):

```spl
index=os sourcetype IN ("chef:compliance", "puppet:report", "ansible:result") earliest=-24h
| lookup cui_hosts.csv hostname AS host OUTPUT cui_flag
| where cui_flag="true"
| eval drift=if(control_status="failed" OR status="changed" OR result="non-compliant", 1, 0)
| stats sum(drift) AS drift_count count AS total_checks latest(_time) AS last_check BY host, profile
| eval drift_pct=round(100*drift_count/total_checks, 1)
| where drift_count > 0
| sort - drift_pct
| table host, profile, total_checks, drift_count, drift_pct, last_check
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm expected results against known CUI systems. Verify lookup tables are populated and field extractions are correct.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Schedule the search at an appropriate interval. Route findings to the compliance ticketing queue. Table of hosts with drift percentage. Heat map of drift by profile. Single value for total hosts with active drift.

## SPL

```spl
index=os sourcetype IN ("chef:compliance", "puppet:report", "ansible:result") earliest=-24h
| lookup cui_hosts.csv hostname AS host OUTPUT cui_flag
| where cui_flag="true"
| eval drift=if(control_status="failed" OR status="changed" OR result="non-compliant", 1, 0)
| stats sum(drift) AS drift_count count AS total_checks latest(_time) AS last_check BY host, profile
| eval drift_pct=round(100*drift_count/total_checks, 1)
| where drift_count > 0
| sort - drift_pct
| table host, profile, total_checks, drift_count, drift_pct, last_check
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table of hosts with drift percentage. Heat map of drift by profile. Single value for total hosts with active drift.
